faqs.q: What does anorexia nervosa actually mean?
faqs.a: Anorexia nervosa is a serious eating disorder centered on an intense fear of gaining weight and a distorted experience of one's own body. These fears drive food restriction that can seriously harm the body over time. It's a recognized mental health condition, not a lifestyle choice.

It can be life threatening, which is why it's taken so seriously, and it carries real medical risk affecting many of the body's systems. It's also treatable, especially when help comes early, with care that usually combines medical support, nutrition, and therapy.

faqs.q: Can you have anorexia without looking underweight?
faqs.a: Yes. Anorexia isn't always visible, and a person can be seriously ill without an obvious change that others notice. The disorder isn't defined by any single appearance.
